title: Varronia mariquitensis
taxonomy: Varronia mariquitensis is classified in the family Boraginaceae. This classification places it within the order Boraginales and the clade asterids. The Boraginaceae family comprises over 1900 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. As a member of this family, Varronia mariquitensis shares characteristics with other plants that have similar growth habits and flower structures. Further classification within the kingdom Plantae and the domain Eukarya reflects its status as a plant species.

intro: Varronia mariquitensis, also known as "mariquit," is a small, deciduous shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Varronia mariquitensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, mariquit is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat fever, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, while also being used as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent.
